text: Code refactoring is the process of improving the internal structure of code without changing what it does. Instead of adding new features, developers clean up existing code to make it clearer and easier to work with. This often means renaming confusing variables, breaking long functions into smaller parts, or removing duplicate logic. The goal is to improve readability—making the code easier for other developers (or yourself in the future) to understand and modify. Refactoring also helps reduce technical debt, which is the cost of keeping messy or inefficient code. By regularly refactoring, you prevent bugs and keep the project healthy, even as it grows.
